concerns.text: Task T020 (Implement classifier) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T019 (Generate training data) to provide the input file `classifier_training_data.json`. A task cannot be parallel-safe if it requires the output of another task to exist before it can run. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule.

Task T024 (Implement runner) is marked [P] but depends on T020 (Classifier) and T045 (Refactor split logic). The description explicitly states 'Dependency: Depends on T020... and T045'. Marking a task as [P] when it has hard dependencies on other implementation tasks is a semantic ordering violation.

Task T034 (Compute gap closure) is marked [P] but depends on T028 (Experiment logs). The description states 'Dependency: This task depends on T028... it is NOT parallel-safe'. The [P] tag is incorrect and misleading for the execution engine.

Task T038 (Stats workflow) is marked [P] but depends on T034 (Metrics) and T009/T041 (Scope/Memory reports). The description does not explicitly list dependencies in the text, but the logic requires the output of T034. Marking it [P] implies it can run before T034, which is impossible.

Task T045 (Refactor split logic) is marked [P] but depends on T019 (which defines the function). The description says 'Refactor... to explicitly import... from code/data/generator.py (defined in T019)'. It cannot run in parallel with T019; it must follow it.

Task T050 (Refactor prompts) is marked [P] but depends on T028 (which logs the state) and T024 (which uses the prompt). While T050 defines the template, the task description implies it updates `runner.py` (T024) to inject the template. If T050 modifies the runner, it must precede T024. If it only defines the template, it should be grouped with T023. The current [P] tag suggests it can run independently of the runner implementation, which is ambiguous and risky for ordering.

Task T054 (Update stats correction) is marked [P] but depends on T038 (Stats workflow) to update the logic. The description says 'Update code/analysis/stats.py'. This is a modification of the task in T038. It cannot be parallel to T038; it must be a sequential refinement or part of T038.
